name: Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop
name_original: Desde La Cazalla de la Sierra hasta Almadén y Santa Olalla del Cala – circular por el Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla
description: This difficult 75.1-mile road cycling loop through Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park offers diverse scenery and 6092 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Cazalla De La Sierra offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The region features gently rolling hills covered in evergreen oaks, cork, and holm oak forests, interspersed with meadows (dehesas) and olive groves. This semi-mountainous terrain provides varied gradients suitable for different fitness levels, from moderate climbs to challenging routes with significant elevation gain. Best road cycling routes around Cazalla De La Sierra The most popular road cycling route is Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop, a 75.1 miles (120.9 km) trail that takes 6 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Isla Margarita Recreation Area – Cazalla de la Sierra loop from Cazalla - Constantina, a difficult 44.8 miles (72.0 km) path. This loop offers a challenging ride with notable climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Cazalla de la Sierra – Ribera del Huéznar Road loop from Cazalla de la Sierra, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through the Ribera del Huéznar area, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Cazalla De La Sierra is defined by rolling hills, dehesas, and river valleys like the Huéznar. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding ascents. The routes in Cazalla De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cazalla De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cazalla de la Sierra?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Cazalla de la Sierra, offering diverse experiences within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Cazalla de la Sierra?
A: The region features gently rolling hills covered in evergreen oaks, cork, and holm oak forests, interspersed with meadows (dehesas) and olive groves. You'll encounter semi-mountainous terrain with varied gradients, including river valleys like the Huéznar, and scenic stretches through traditional Iberian landscapes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Cazalla de la Sierra?
A: The road cycling routes in Cazalla de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas of the dramatic former iron mines and karst formations, which are also a designated National Heritage site.

Q: Can I cycle past any waterfalls in the region?
A: Absolutely. The Huéznar River is known for its scenic cascades. You can find routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which have been declared a Natural Monument, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options?
A: While many road cycling routes involve significant elevation, the Sierra Norte Greenway offers a flatter, traffic-free alternative. This linear path, utilizing a former railway line, is excellent for cycling and connects to areas like San Nicolás del Puerto, making it suitable for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31402205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazalla de la Sierra – Isla Margarita Recreation Area loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 53.3 km ride, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazalla de la Sierra – Ribera del Huéznar Road loop&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter 47.8 km option.

Q: What natural features can I explore while cycling?
A: Beyond the waterfalls, you can explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, a UNESCO Global Geopark. This includes the lush riverside woodlands of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/736496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar River Landscape&lt;/a&gt;, the unique rock formations of El Cerro del Hierro, and the expansive dehesas with their iconic evergreen oak trees.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Cazalla de la Sierra?
A: Cazalla de la Sierra is generally suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while winters are typically mild. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, the region offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993987296/sierra-morena-de-sevilla-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 121 km trail with substantial elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40307418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Margarita Recreation Area – Huéznar Waterfalls loop&lt;/a&gt; covers over 100 km with significant climbs.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Cazalla de la Sierra?
A: Cazalla de la Sierra, as a central hub, typically offers various parking options within the town itself, providing convenient access to the start points of many routes. Specific parking areas may also be available near popular recreation spots or trailheads within the natural park.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to visit near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Cazalla de la Sierra itself boasts a rich heritage with historic architecture, including the Cartuja de Cazalla. The region is also known for its production of anise and cherry liqueur, with local distilleries offering tastings, providing a cultural experience alongside your ride.
tips: Views of the Melonares reservoir, which was built under the pressure of the 1992-1996 drought.
Beautiful road with nice views and little traffic.
The asphalt is not the best (at least that was the case in 2013), but it is an extremely low-traffic road through the national park. Very nice bike tour.
Cazalla de la Sierra is a beautiful town with a lot of history. Worth a visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling loop is approximately 120.86 km long and typically takes around 6 hours and 17 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop is rated as difficult. It features significant elevation gain, with approximately 1856 meters of climbing, making it suitable for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this loop?
faqs.answer: While there isn't one single designated road cycling trail, this loop primarily uses paved roads within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. You can expect sections with good asphalt and winding turns, but some stretches may be narrower with varying surface quality, including potential deterioration or potholes. The broader region also includes the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte de Sevilla, which offers an 18.27 km asphalt surface ideal for road cycling.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers diverse scenery. You'll pass by the View of Melonares Reservoir, experience the scenic Almadén de la Plata to Santa Olalla del Cala Road, and traverse parts of the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park. The route also goes through picturesque settlements like Cazalla de la Sierra.

faqs.question: Are there any unique natural features to look out for?
faqs.answer: Yes, the region is known for its rich natural beauty. Along routes like the Vía Verde, you might encounter gallery forests and crystal-clear waters alongside the Ribera del Huéznar. A notable feature is the unique karst landscape of Cerro del Hierro, an old mining area with distinctive geological formations. Other attractions include the 'beach' of San Nicolás del Puerto and the Huéznar waterfalls.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to road cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Morena de Sevilla region generally experiences hot summers and mild winters. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are typically the most pleasant seasons for road cycling, offering comfortable temperatures and beautiful landscapes.

faqs.question: Where does the Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop start and end, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour description indicates the loop starts from La Cazalla de la Sierra. Parking availability in villages like Cazalla de la Sierra would typically be in public parking areas. For specific trailhead parking, it's advisable to check local maps or resources for the starting point of your chosen segment within the park.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for cycling in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and designated routes within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas or private land. For specific events or large groups, it's wise to check with park authorities.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop?
faqs.answer: While the loop primarily follows public roads, specific regulations for dogs may apply in certain natural areas or villages within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. If you plan to stop or venture off the main roads, it's best to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with several other paths. Notably, about 30% of the route overlaps with the Transandalus trail, and 16% with the Sendero de Sierra Morena. Smaller portions also coincide with the Mtb Vía de la Plata and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop passes through or near several picturesque villages such as Cazalla de la Sierra, Constantina, and Alanís. These towns typically offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options where cyclists can refuel and rest.
